Questions 77-80 refer to the following investigation.

A student placed equal initial populations of duckweed plants in culture dishes containing different growth media. The dishes were kept under the same light and temperature conditions for 15 days. The number of duckweed plants in each dish was counted every 5 days.

| Distilled water | 3 | 4 | 6 | 7 |
| Pond water | 3 | 5 | 6 | 8 |
| Pond water with added chlorophyll | 3 | 6 | 9 | 12 |
| Nitrate-enriched distilled water | 3 | 8 | 13 | 18 |
| N-P-K fertilizer solution | 3 | 10 | 17 | 24 |

options.D: The frequency of the Ellis-van Creveld allele is 0.0447 in the isolated population and 0.0026 in the general population, which suggests that genetic drift has occurred in the isolated population.

subject: AP Biology
text: Two heterozygous parents have a child with a recessive phenotype. Which inheritance idea explains this outcome? Case 1.
